seoTitle: Hay vs Hayes Gap — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hay and Hayes Gap. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hay vs Hayes Gap: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hay and Hayes Gap.
heading: Hay vs Hayes Gap — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hay, New South Wales and Hayes Gap, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hayes Gap has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Hay (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hay or Hayes Gap safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hay has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Hayes Gap scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hay scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Hayes Gap scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hay or Hayes Gap?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hayes Gap has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
